meta_title: Impact Doors in The Acreage, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in The Acreage, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, financing from $87/mo. Protect your Palm Beach County home today.
h1: Impact Doors for The Acreage Homes — Built for Palm Beach County's Toughest Storms
intro:
The Acreage is one of Palm Beach County's most unique communities — a sprawling, semi-rural enclave of larger lots and custom homes where neighbors value their independence, their land, and their privacy. From the quiet lanes off Hamlin Boulevard to the established properties along Seminole Pratt Whitney Road and the family neighborhoods tucked into Indian Trail, homeowners here have invested heavily in properties that average around $430,000 in value. Protecting that investment starts at the front door — literally. Impact doors aren't just a smart upgrade for The Acreage; they're increasingly becoming a necessity as storm seasons grow more unpredictable and insurance premiums continue to climb across Palm Beach County.

Unlike coastal communities that sit directly on the shoreline, The Acreage sits roughly 20 miles inland — and many homeowners assume that distance provides adequate protection. It doesn't. The area falls within a 140 mph wind zone, and when major hurricanes make landfall or track across South Florida, the winds, flying debris, and storm surge effects can be devastating even this far from the water. Standard doors — even newer ones — are not engineered to withstand the kind of sustained winds and projectile impact that a major storm can produce. Impact-resistant entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors manufactured by ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI are specifically designed and tested to handle exactly those conditions, providing a sealed, fortified barrier that keeps your family safe and your home intact.

With approximately 20% of homes in The Acreage built before 1994 — before Florida's modern hurricane construction standards were adopted — the need for door upgrades is especially significant. Older door frames, single-point locking systems, and non-laminated glass panels simply don't meet today's Florida Building Code requirements. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings over 60 years of combined installation experience to every project in The Acreage, and our team knows precisely what it takes to bring your home up to code while delivering the lasting performance and curb appeal you deserve.

section_why.h2: Why The Acreage Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Impact Door Installation
section_why.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've earned the title 'The Kings of Service' by doing things the right way — every time. One of the most important ways we deliver on that promise is through our 100% in-house installation model. We don't use subcontractors, day laborers, or rotating crews pulled from staffing agencies. Every impact door we install in The Acreage is handled by our own trained, experienced technicians who understand the nuances of local construction, soil conditions, and the structural demands of homes throughout Palm Beach County. With over 500 homes completed across the region and an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau, our track record speaks for itself.

Installing an impact door isn't as simple as swapping out a standard door. The process requires precise measurements, proper frame preparation, code-compliant anchoring, and professional sealing to ensure the door performs as rated during a storm event. Improper installation — the kind you risk when a company uses unfamiliar subcontractors — can void manufacturer warranties, fail building inspections, and leave your home dangerously vulnerable even with a premium product in place. Our crews have installed hundreds of impact entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors across communities like The Acreage, Royal Palm Beach, Loxahatchee, and Wellington, and that local familiarity makes a measurable difference in project quality and timeline.

We also handle every permit through the Palm Beach County Building Division on your behalf, so you never have to navigate the paperwork, fees, or inspection scheduling on your own. From the initial consultation and free measurement to the final inspection sign-off, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire process. Our team will walk you through product options, help you understand what your specific home requires under current code, and ensure that every door we install comes with the documentation your insurance company needs to process any applicable discounts or credits. When you choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for your impact doors in The Acreage, you're not just buying a product — you're getting a complete, professionally managed service experience from a company that genuinely cares about getting it right.

section_history.h2: Storm History Proves The Acreage Needs Hurricane-Rated Impact Doors
section_history.body:
The Acreage has a storm history that should give every homeowner pause. Since 1990, the community has been affected by at least six named storms, with three of the most significant being Hurricane Frances in 2004, Hurricane Wilma in 2005, and Hurricane Irma in 2017. Frances was a slow-moving Category 2 storm that battered Palm Beach County for hours, producing sustained winds that caused widespread structural damage across the region. The prolonged exposure — far longer than a fast-moving storm — put tremendous stress on doors, windows, and roofing systems that weren't designed to hold up under continuous hurricane-force conditions.

Just a year later, Hurricane Wilma roared across South Florida as one of the fastest-moving and most intense hurricanes to affect the region in decades. Wilma's rapid intensification and powerful winds left a broad swath of damage through Palm Beach County, and communities like The Acreage experienced the kind of sudden, violent wind gusts that shatter standard glass, blow in doors, and create dangerous pressure differentials inside homes. The interior pressure surge that follows a failed door or window is one of the leading causes of roof loss during major hurricanes — a sobering fact that underscores why a fortified door is about much more than entry point security.

Hurricane Irma in 2017 served as the most recent reminder of how real the threat remains for inland communities. Though Irma made its Florida landfall on the Gulf Coast, its massive wind field affected virtually all of South Florida, including The Acreage. Homes that had impact doors and windows in place fared dramatically better than those that relied on plywood or older standard installations. These three storms alone represent a compelling argument for making the investment in ESW and CWI impact doors before the next major hurricane season arrives.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in The Acreage and Palm Beach County
section_code.body:
The Acreage falls under Palm Beach County jurisdiction and is subject to the Florida Building Code (FBC), which includes specific requirements for windborne debris protection in all construction and replacement projects. While The Acreage is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies primarily to Miami-Dade and Broward counties — it is still situated in a 140 mph wind design zone. This means that any impact door installed in The Acreage must be tested and rated to withstand those wind speeds and must comply with all applicable sections of the current Florida Building Code for wind-borne debris resistance.

For homeowners replacing existing doors or installing new ones, permits are required through the Palm Beach County Building Division. These permits ensure that the products used are approved for the local wind zone, that the installation is performed correctly, and that a licensed inspector verifies compliance before the project is considered complete. Skipping the permit process isn't just a legal risk — it can create serious problems when you go to sell your home or file an insurance claim. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles all permitting documentation on your behalf, working directly with the Palm Beach County Building Division to ensure every installation meets or exceeds code requirements.

For homes built before 1994, the permitting and inspection process may also trigger requirements to bring other elements of the home into compliance — something our team will discuss transparently during your consultation. Products from ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI that we install are fully approved under the Florida Building Code and carry the Miami-Dade and Florida Product Approval numbers necessary for permit applications. Whether you're upgrading a single entry door or replacing all exterior doors throughout your home, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing ensures every installation is fully code-compliant and properly documented.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premier-Grade Protection for Every Entry Point in Your Acreage Home
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors from two industry-leading brands — ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI — both of which are engineered specifically for the demanding conditions found in South Florida's hurricane-prone climate. These aren't off-the-shelf products adapted for storm use; they're purpose-built impact systems that have been rigorously tested and certified to meet Florida Building Code requirements for the 140 mph wind zone that covers The Acreage. Whether you're looking to upgrade your front entry door, replace aging sliding glass doors that open to a back patio, or install elegant French doors that maintain the aesthetic of your custom home, we carry the right product for your specific application.

ESW impact entry doors feature laminated safety glass, heavy-gauge aluminum or fiberglass frames, and multi-point locking systems that distribute force evenly across the entire door rather than concentrating stress at a single deadbolt point. This multi-point locking design is critical during high-wind events — it significantly reduces the chance of door failure under sustained pressure. CWI sliding glass impact doors offer a sleek, low-profile option for homeowners who want unobstructed backyard views without sacrificing protection. Their reinforced track systems, interlocking panels, and impact-rated glass meet the same stringent performance standards as our entry door systems, making them a popular choice for The Acreage homes with covered patios and pool enclosures.

Both ESW and CWI products also deliver significant energy efficiency benefits alongside their storm protection capabilities. The laminated glass and thermally broken frame systems help reduce heat transfer, keeping your home cooler during Florida's brutally hot summers and reducing the load on your air conditioning system. Many homeowners in The Acreage report noticeable reductions in their energy bills after replacing older doors with impact-rated systems from these brands. Combined with the security, noise reduction, and code compliance benefits, ESW and CWI impact doors represent one of the most comprehensive home improvement investments available to Palm Beach County residents.

section_cost.h2: What Do Impact Doors Cost in The Acreage — And How Can You Finance the Investment?
section_cost.body:
For homeowners in The Acreage, where the average property value hovers around $430,000, investing in impact doors is not only a safety decision — it's a smart financial one. The cost of impact door installation varies depending on the number of doors, door type (entry, sliding glass, or French), frame material, glass configuration, and any structural modifications required by the existing opening. As a general guideline, homeowners can expect to invest anywhere from $1,500 to $4,000 or more per door installed, with complete whole-home packages (covering all exterior doors) typically ranging from $5,000 to $15,000 depending on the scope of work. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides free, no-obligation estimates that give you a transparent, itemized breakdown with no hidden fees.

To make this investment accessible for middle-income households throughout The Acreage, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down available for qualified buyers. This means you can get fully code-compliant, professionally installed impact doors in place before hurricane season without draining your savings or delaying the protection your family needs. Our financing partners offer competitive rates and flexible terms designed to work with a variety of budgets and credit profiles, and our team can walk you through the options during your in-home consultation.

From a return-on-investment standpoint, impact doors in The Acreage make strong financial sense beyond the upfront cost. Many homeowners recoup a meaningful portion of their investment through reduced homeowner's insurance premiums — sometimes seeing annual savings of several hundred dollars or more depending on their carrier and current coverage. Additionally, impact doors are a recognized value-adding upgrade in the Palm Beach County real estate market, appealing to buyers who prioritize safety and low maintenance. When you factor in energy savings, insurance discounts, and increased home value, the true cost of impact door installation is substantially lower than the sticker price suggests.

section_insurance.h2: Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance Premiums with Impact Doors in The Acreage
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediate and tangible financial benefits of installing impact doors in The Acreage is the potential reduction in your homeowner's insurance premiums. Florida's insurance market has been under significant stress in recent years, with many carriers raising rates, reducing coverage, or exiting the state entirely. For Palm Beach County homeowners, having a fully documented wind mitigation upgrade — including impact-rated doors — can make a meaningful difference in both the cost and availability of coverage. Insurance carriers operating in Florida are required to offer wind mitigation credits for homes that meet specific protection standards, and impact doors with approved ratings are a qualifying upgrade.

The wind mitigation inspection process is the key to unlocking these discounts. After Premier Impact Windows & Roofing completes your impact door installation, you can request a wind mitigation inspection from a licensed inspector, who will document the upgraded opening protections on a standardized OIR-B1-1802 Wind Mitigation form. This form is then submitted to your insurance company, which uses it to calculate applicable credits. Depending on how many openings in your home are now protected with impact-rated products and your current carrier's rating methodology, annual premium reductions of 10% to 30% or more are achievable for Palm Beach County homeowners. Over five to ten years, those savings can add up to thousands of dollars — significantly offsetting the original installation cost.

Common carriers serving The Acreage area, including Citizens Property Insurance Corporation, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Property & Casualty Insurance, all recognize wind mitigation credits as part of their rating process. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides complete documentation of all installed products — including Florida Product Approval numbers, NOA (Notice of Acceptance) documentation where applicable, and installation permits — so your wind mitigation inspector has everything needed to complete the report accurately. Our team can also guide you through the process of sharing this documentation with your insurance agent to ensure you receive every credit you've earned.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors in The Acreage if I'm 20 miles from the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. The Acreage sits in a 140 mph wind design zone under the Florida Building Code, meaning storms can produce dangerous wind speeds and flying debris even this far inland. Hurricanes Frances, Wilma, and Irma all caused significant damage in the area. Distance from the coast does not eliminate hurricane risk — impact doors provide essential protection regardless of your proximity to the shoreline.

faqs.q: How much does impact door installation cost in The Acreage, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Costs vary based on the number of doors, type (entry, sliding, or French), and scope of work, but most installations range from $1,500 to $4,000+ per door. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified buyers. We provide free, no-obligation estimates with transparent, itemized pricing so you know exactly what you're investing in before any work begins.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my doors with impact doors in The Acreage?
faqs.a: Yes. Any impact door installation in The Acreage requires a permit through the Palm Beach County Building Division. This ensures the products used are approved for the local 140 mph wind zone and that the installation is properly inspected. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — from application to final inspection — so you don't have to navigate the bureaucracy yourself.

faqs.q: How long does it take to have impact doors installed in my Acreage home?
faqs.a: The process from consultation to completed installation typically takes three to six weeks, depending on product lead times, permit processing through Palm Beach County, and your installation schedule. The physical installation of most impact doors takes one to two days for our in-house crew. We'll keep you informed throughout every step of the timeline so there are no surprises.

faqs.q: Will impact doors lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Palm Beach County?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. Florida insurance carriers are required to offer wind mitigation credits for homes with impact-rated door and window systems. After installation, a licensed wind mitigation inspector documents your upgraded openings on the OIR-B1-1802 form, which is submitted to your carrier for applicable discounts. Palm Beach County homeowners often see annual savings of 10% to 30% or more on their wind coverage premiums — potentially saving thousands of dollars over the life of the product.
